title: Add reference track and colored mismatches
description: Use --reference FASTA to draw a visible reference-base track and color read bases that differ from the reference by nucleotide identity.
close_reason: Added FASTA-backed reference track and reference-aware mismatch coloring with tests and docs.

title: Stabilize feature track rendering
description: Exon models can flicker while navigating because grouped feature model ordering is not fully deterministic across render passes. Make feature row ordering stable and avoid suppressing unrelated gene records.
close_reason: Feature track row ordering is deterministic and grouped exon models no longer hide unrelated gene rows.

title: Render transcript exon models
description: Render grouped transcript models with intron backbones and exon/CDS/UTR blocks on the same row, so exon-intron structure is easier to read than independent feature records.
close_reason: Grouped transcript models now render intron backbones and exon/CDS/UTR blocks on the same row, with tests for grouping and duplicate suppression

title: Clarify exon and intron rendering
description: Make the annotation feature track distinguish exon blocks from intron/transcript connector lines instead of rendering all features with the same arrow body.
close_reason: Feature track now renders transcript/gene backbones as intron lines with strand ticks and exons/CDS/UTR as distinct blocks

title: Support plain and gzipped GTF/GFF loading
description: Load annotation files from plain text or gzip/BGZF streams and parse both GFF3 key=value and GTF key "value" attributes so --gff works with common .gtf.gz files.
close_reason: Plain and gzip/BGZF GFF3/GTF loading implemented with GTF attributes and tests

title: Fix TUI panic on unchecked render/event paths
description: The terminal browser can panic with index out of bounds during interactive use. Harden unchecked render indices and fix event handling so one poll consumes one event.
close_reason: Fixed unchecked render/event paths and verified with cargo fmt/test

title: GFF3: parser, feature store, gene jump
description: Add noodles-gff, parse GFF3 into feature index, jump-to-gene by name/ID, render features track in TUI
close_reason: Closed

title: Core modules: cli, app, error, region
description: Scaffold cli.rs, app.rs, error.rs, region.rs with types and parsers
close_reason: Closed

title: Cargo.toml: add all dependencies
description: Add noodles, ratatui, crossterm, clap, anyhow, thiserror to Cargo.toml
close_reason: Closed

title: Use shared-gap insertion expansion
description: Insertion expansion should match IGV-style behavior: expanding an insertion creates a shared gap at the insertion locus, shifting all rendered alignments after the break consistently instead of overlaying inserted bases on one row or shifting each read independently.
acceptance_criteria: Manual insertion expansion creates a shared visual gap at an insertion locus. All read rows use the same shifted coordinate mapping after the selected locus. Inserted bases render in the gap only for reads with sequence at that insertion. Collapsed mode remains a prominent insertion marker. Tests cover shared shifting and non-carrier gap behavior.
close_reason: Implemented shared-gap insertion expansion so all tracks/read rows shift consistently at the selected insertion locus.

title: Refine insertion visibility without alignment shifting
description: Expanded insertion rendering currently shifts downstream aligned bases, which makes alignments look out of register. Collapsed insertion markers are also too subtle. Refine insertion rendering so expansion does not shift downstream alignment columns and collapsed mode emphasizes insertion boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Expanded insertion rendering does not shift downstream aligned bases. Collapsed insertion markers are more visually prominent than the current caret-only marker. Boundary bases around insertions remain emphasized. Renderer tests cover collapsed and expanded insertion behavior.
close_reason: Refined insertion rendering to avoid downstream alignment shifts and use a stronger collapsed insertion marker.

title: Add manual insertion expansion toggle
description: Expanded insertion rendering currently activates automatically at high zoom. Make insertions collapsed by default and add a manual toggle so users can expand inserted sequence in the current view only when requested.
acceptance_criteria: Insertions are collapsed by default. A keyboard command toggles expanded insertion rendering. Help and status text document the toggle. Existing insertion overwrite regression remains covered by tests.
close_reason: Added manual i-key insertion expansion toggle, collapsed default rendering, UI indicators, and tests.

title: Expand insertion rendering in current view
description: Insertions are currently collapsed to a caret and can be overwritten by adjacent aligned bases. Add current-view insertion expansion so inserted bases are visible and insertion boundary bases are emphasized with bold underline styling.
acceptance_criteria: Insertion sequence is rendered between the aligned boundary bases when base-level view has enough columns. Boundary bases flanking an insertion are bold and underlined. Insertion rendering does not get overwritten by following CIGAR operations. Existing non-insertion rendering behavior remains covered by tests.
close_reason: Implemented expanded base-level insertion rendering with bold underlined boundaries and regression tests.

title: Rename crate package to locus-tui
description: The crates.io package name locus is unavailable. Rename the publishable package to locus-tui while keeping the installed binary command as locus.
close_reason: Renamed publishable package to locus-tui while keeping the installed binary command as locus.

title: Use tabix-indexed annotation queries
description: When a sorted BGZF-compressed GFF/GTF has a tabix index, query visible regions through the index instead of scanning every loaded feature.
notes: Implemented indexed visible-region queries when a .tbi sidecar exists for a gzip/BGZF GFF/GTF. The store still builds the existing in-memory feature/name index for feature search; rendering no longer scans that vector when tabix query succeeds.
close_reason: Feature rendering now queries .tbi-indexed BGZF annotations for visible regions, with fallback to the prior linear scan and an indexed fixture test.

title: Add terminal screenshot capture
description: Add an in-app keybinding to save the current ratatui screen as a timestamped text screenshot file for sharing/debugging.
close_reason: Added s keybinding to save current ratatui screen to screenshots/locus-<timestamp>.txt and documented it

title: Clarify TUI top status bar
description: Make the top bar easier to scan by using consistent labeled fields and avoiding crowded status text.
close_reason: Top status bar now uses consistent labels, explicit scale/read metrics, and truncation for narrow terminals

title: Render model and pileup layout
description: cache.rs + render/: RenderRead, row-packing, coverage binning
close_reason: Closed

title: BAM source: open file, index, header
description: bam.rs: BamSource struct, open BAM+index, parse header, fetch reads
close_reason: Closed

title: Clean up cargo clippy warnings
close_reason: Resolved cargo clippy warnings across app/cache/gff/render/main. Verified cargo fmt --all, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features with zero warnings, cargo test, and cargo check.

title: Bump crate version for recent UI features
close_reason: Bumped locus-tui package and lockfile versions to 0.2.0 after recent user-visible UI features. Verified cargo fmt --all, cargo check, and cargo test.

title: Add light mode rendering style
description: Add a light-background style option so terminal rendering and screenshots can show reads, methylation, insertions, features, and UI bars legibly on light backgrounds.
acceptance_criteria: Users can switch between default dark styling and light mode. Methylation, insertion, deletion, feature, and reference/read colors remain distinguishable. README/demo documentation mentions the light mode option. cargo fmt/check/test pass.
close_reason: Implemented light theme with --light startup flag, t runtime toggle, themed render tracks/screenshots/docs/demo captures. Verified cargo fmt --all, cargo check, cargo test, and cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features (warnings only).

title: Add demo dataset and README screenshots
description: Create a small committed example dataset plus rendered screenshots so README sections can showcase insertion expansion, methylation display, annotations, and screenshot/export behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Repository includes a small demo dataset or reproducible demo generator. README references feature-specific screenshots/artifacts and commands. Local cargo fmt/check/test pass before PR.
close_reason: Added a small demo dataset, reproducible build/capture scripts, app-generated screenshot artifacts, README image, and feature-specific README notes.

title: Add GitHub Actions Rust test workflow
description: Add repository CI so pull requests and branch pushes validate formatting/build/tests for the Rust TUI before merge.
acceptance_criteria: A GitHub Actions workflow runs cargo fmt --check, cargo check, and cargo test on pull requests and pushes. The workflow can also be run manually. Local cargo test passes before commit.
close_reason: Added Rust GitHub Actions workflow for fmt, check, and tests; local CI-equivalent gates pass.

title: Add methylation toggle UI
description: Add normal-mode UI state and keybinding for toggling methylation display on reads. The toggle should be discoverable in the bottom bar/help overlay and should not conflict with existing insertion expansion or feature-search controls.
design: Use a mnemonic key that does not conflict with current normal-mode controls. Candidate: m for methylation. Keep insertion expansion on i/Tab.
acceptance_criteria: Normal mode has a documented methylation toggle key. Top or bottom UI reflects whether methylation display is enabled. Toggle state does not refetch reads unnecessarily. Existing modes keep their current key behavior. UI/event tests or focused app-state tests cover the toggle.
close_reason: Implemented normal-mode methylation toggle UI on m with top/bottom/help discoverability and tests.

title: Validate methylation toggle with fixtures
description: Add test fixtures and coverage for methylation parsing, CIGAR mapping, rendering, and user-facing toggle behavior. Include at least one realistic MM/ML example and edge cases without modified-base tags.
design: Prefer small synthetic records or parser-level fixtures for MM/ML decoding, and renderer-level synthetic RenderRead values for visual tests. Avoid requiring large BAM fixtures unless a tiny indexed test BAM is already practical.
acceptance_criteria: Tests include reads with MM+ML, MM without ML, no methylation tags, reverse-strand reads, and CIGARs with insertion/deletion/soft clip. cargo test passes. README/help text documents the methylation toggle and any supported tag limitations.
close_reason: Added parser fixtures for empty and reverse-strand MM tags, renderer methylation fixtures, and README/help documentation; cargo test passes.

title: Parse MM/ML modified-base tags
description: Add BAM auxiliary tag parsing for SAM MM and ML tags so read methylation calls are available before rendering. Focus first on cytosine methylation calls commonly emitted by ONT/PacBio pipelines, while leaving the parser extensible for other modified bases.
design: Keep parsing isolated from terminal rendering, likely in bam.rs or a small methylation/modbase module. Convert probabilities to a compact u8 probability or normalized score. Do not assume every read has ML.
acceptance_criteria: MM tags are parsed for canonical base, strand, modification code, skip mode, and delta positions. ML probabilities are associated with parsed calls when present. Unsupported or malformed tags fail gracefully without dropping the read. Unit tests cover representative MM/ML examples and missing ML tags.
close_reason: Implemented MM/ML modified-base parsing, wired parsed calls into RenderRead, and added parser tests for probabilities, missing ML, skip modes, malformed tags, and out-of-range deltas.

title: Render methylation marks on reads
description: Add read-track rendering for methylation calls when methylation display is enabled. The visual style should be legible in terminal and HTML screenshots while preserving existing base colors and mismatch styling as much as practical.
design: Prefer background or modifier cues that do not erase the base letter. Avoid a one-note color palette; choose a methylation color that remains distinguishable from mismatch, insertion, deletion, and MAPQ dimming styles.
acceptance_criteria: Methylated and unmethylated calls have distinct visual treatments. Probability/confidence can be represented by intensity or a small set of bins. Rendering composes with reference mismatch styling and indel expansion. Screenshot HTML/ANSI output preserves the selected styles. Renderer tests cover styled methylated and unmethylated bases.
close_reason: Rendered methylation calls on read bases when enabled, with probability-dependent styling and insertion-expansion tests.

title: Map methylation calls through CIGAR
description: Map parsed modified-base calls from read coordinates onto aligned reference positions so the pileup renderer can show methylation on the correct visible base. The mapping must respect matches, mismatches, insertions, deletions, skips, and soft clips.
design: Reuse the existing CIGAR walk that already tracks read_pos/ref_pos in render logic, but perform methylation coordinate mapping before rendering so the renderer can look up calls by reference/read position cheaply.
acceptance_criteria: Modified-base calls on aligned read bases receive reference positions. Calls on inserted or soft-clipped bases are either retained with read-coordinate metadata or intentionally skipped with tests documenting the behavior. Mapping handles reverse-strand reads consistently with decoded sequence orientation. Unit tests cover CIGARs with insertions, deletions, skips, and soft clips.
close_reason: Added RenderRead methylation-to-reference mapping through CIGAR with tests for matches, soft clips, insertions, deletions, and skips.

title: Add read methylation display toggle
description: Plan and implement a read-level methylation display mode. The feature should parse modified-base BAM tags, preserve per-read methylation calls in the render model, and let users toggle methylation visualization on reads without disrupting existing base, mismatch, insertion, deletion, and feature rendering.
design: Use SAM/BAM MM/ML modified-base tags as the source of methylation calls. Store decoded calls on RenderRead in read-coordinate form plus enough metadata to render on reference-aligned bases. Rendering should be opt-in and should compose with existing mismatch and indel display rather than replacing core read layout.
acceptance_criteria: Users can toggle methylation display from normal mode. Reads with supported methylation tags show methylation state/probability in the pileup. Reads without methylation tags render unchanged. The feature is covered by parser, coordinate mapping, renderer, and UI tests.
close_reason: Implemented MM/ML parsing, CIGAR mapping, m-key UI toggle, read rendering, docs, and test coverage.

title: Cycle expanded insertion locus with Tab
description: Insertion expansion currently selects one visible insertion automatically. Add a normal-mode Tab shortcut to move the expanded shared-gap view to the next visible insertion locus, with Shift+Tab moving backward.
acceptance_criteria: Tab cycles to the next visible expanded insertion locus when insertion expansion is enabled. Shift+Tab cycles backward. The selected locus persists during rendering while visible. Status text reports the selected insertion position. Tests cover visible insertion gap ordering and selection behavior.
close_reason: Added normal-mode Tab/Shift+Tab cycling for the selected expanded insertion locus.

title: Box expanded insertion gaps
description: Expanded insertion gaps should be visually boxed so inserted sequence is distinguishable from deletions or empty gap cells. Add border columns around the shared insertion gap when expansion is active.
acceptance_criteria: Expanded insertion gaps reserve visible left and right border columns. Inserted bases render inside the bordered gap. Non-carrier reads show the same boxed gap region without inserted bases. Tests cover boxed expanded insertion rendering.
close_reason: Added bracketed border columns around expanded insertion gaps and removed gray inserted-base background.

title: Polish crate metadata for publishing
description: Cargo.toml has initial crates.io metadata that should be made accurate and committed without including local .codex files.
close_reason: Added publishing metadata, excluded local .codex files, and verified package contents.

title: Prepare annotation files for indexed browsing
description: Add an explicit workflow to sort, BGZF-compress, and tabix-index GFF3/GTF annotations when they are not already prepared.
close_reason: Added prepare-annotations subcommand that sorts GFF/GTF records, writes BGZF output, creates a .tbi index, and validates the prepared file through indexed region queries.

title: Polish screenshot export filenames and docs
description: Avoid timestamp collisions when saving screenshots and update documentation to reflect both ANSI text and HTML screenshot outputs.
close_reason: Screenshot filenames now include millisecond precision plus process id, and README describes both text and HTML outputs

title: Export HTML screenshots
description: Screenshots should include an HTML artifact that preserves ratatui cell colors in browsers and editors, while keeping the existing text screenshot export.
close_reason: Added HTML screenshot export alongside ANSI text screenshots with tests for styling and escaping.

title: Preserve colors in text screenshots
description: Text screenshots currently write only buffer symbols and discard ratatui cell styles. Emit ANSI SGR escape codes by default so catting a screenshot in an ANSI-aware terminal preserves colors.
close_reason: Text screenshot export now emits ANSI SGR styling by default and tests cover colored output.

title: TUI: layout, widgets, keyboard nav
description: ui.rs, events.rs, layout.rs: ratatui app loop, all keybindings
close_reason: Closed

title: Tests: region parser, pileup, coverage
description: Unit tests for region parser, bp-to-col, row packing, coverage
close_reason: Closed
